payload.name: PID Integrator Windup
payload.symptom_text: Actuator saturates while integral term keeps accumulating; recovery after setpoint change is slow.
observable_signals: actuator output clipped to limit for many control steps
overshoot grows after error persists
settling time blows up after setpoint reversal
likely_causes: integral gain too high for current actuator range
contraindications: do not simply increase Ki when output is already saturated
do not remove the output limiter

payload.name: Actuator Output Unbounded
payload.symptom_text: Controller command exceeds the physical actuator limit; torque or velocity command grows without bound.
observable_signals: torque command > torque_max
simulator emits saturation warning
hardware E-stop or thermal trip
contraindications: do not raise torque_max to mask the bug

payload.name: KV-cache Unbounded Growth
payload.symptom_text: Long-context inference accumulates an unbounded key/value cache and OOMs.
observable_signals: GPU memory rises linearly with sequence length
throughput collapses past a context threshold
CUDA OOM error
likely_causes: no eviction policy on KV cache
contraindications: do not just shrink batch size — wastes utilization
do not silently truncate without telling the planner

payload.name: Gradient Explosion
payload.symptom_text: Training loss spikes to NaN; gradient norm grows by orders of magnitude in a few steps.
observable_signals: loss curve flips to NaN
grad-norm > 1e3 within a window
reward variance explodes (RL)
contraindications: do not turn off the offending layer wholesale

payload.name: Output Clamp Missing
payload.symptom_text: Final controller command is not bounded before it reaches the actuator interface.
observable_signals: command magnitude tracks raw PID output
actuator saturation flag never set by driver
contraindications: do not implement clamp inside the integrator step — keep it on the output

payload.name: Planning / Trajectory Divergence
payload.symptom_text: Open-loop plan drifts from the realised trajectory; agent does not replan when state deviates.
observable_signals: position error grows monotonically
velocity discontinuity at replan boundary
safety margin shrinks below threshold
contraindications: do not just shorten the horizon — fix the trigger

payload.name: Simulator / Compile Failure
payload.symptom_text: Simulation backend or build step fails transiently (network blip, dep resolver hiccup, GPU contention).
observable_signals: return code != 0 once, succeeds on retry
error string mentions network, timeout, lock
contraindications: do not retry without exponential back-off + jitter — thundering herd

payload.name: PPO Entropy Collapse
payload.symptom_text: Policy entropy decays to near zero too early; agent becomes deterministic before exploration is complete.
observable_signals: mean entropy < threshold within first 10% of training
reward plateaus at a sub-optimal mode
new tasks produce the same action
contraindications: do not just raise lr — re-add entropy bonus first

payload.name: Unvalidated Input Crash
payload.symptom_text: Function receives malformed or out-of-range input and crashes (or silently mis-computes) without a guarding check.
observable_signals: exception traceback originates inside an inner loop
downstream NaN that traces back to a single unchecked value
intermittent failures that disappear when input is hand-curated
likely_causes: implicit assumption about input shape or dtype
no defensive clamp at the boundary
contraindications: do not wrap the whole call in try/except — fail loudly at the boundary instead

payload.name: Runaway Search / No Time Budget
payload.symptom_text: Iterative search runs past its useful budget — either timing out the harness or wasting compute past a flat reward plateau.
observable_signals: wall-clock exceeds verifier timeout
score curve flat for many iterations before exit
per-iteration cost amortises poorly
contraindications: do not pick a budget below the first feasible solution — that aborts before convergence

payload.name: Random Search Inefficiency
payload.symptom_text: Pure random search keeps sampling uninformative points instead of exploiting structure the gradient or surrogate exposes.
observable_signals: improvement rate plateaus while compute keeps going
best-so-far stuck at one of the first 5 samples
no exploration of high-density regions in the trust region
contraindications: do not jump straight to a model-heavy optimiser before checking the search space is structured

payload.name: Python Inner-Loop Overhead
payload.symptom_text: Inner Python loop dominates wall-clock; replacing it with a vectorised primitive recovers an order of magnitude.
observable_signals: profiler shows interpreter overhead > numerical work
throughput scales sub-linearly with batch size
NumPy / Triton primitives unused on a hot path
contraindications: do not vectorise without preserving the original numerical semantics

payload.name: Cold-Start Search Wasted Budget
payload.symptom_text: Search restarts from a random or default seed each call, ignoring prior-best solutions.
observable_signals: first-iteration score worse than last archived best
convergence time longer than necessary
cached results not loaded
contraindications: do not blindly warm-start when the task distribution has shifted

payload.name: AES Byte-Op Overhead
payload.symptom_text: Per-byte AES (sbox + shift + mix as separate ops) is dominated by byte arithmetic; throughput well below memory bandwidth.
observable_signals: throughput stays well below the CPU-memory ceiling
profiler shows arithmetic-unit saturation
likely_causes: sbox and mix-columns implemented as discrete byte ops
contraindications: do not just inline more — the win comes from merging ops, not unrolling them

payload.name: AES Round-Loop Branch Mispredict
payload.symptom_text: Loop over 10 AES rounds keeps a counter live and exposes a predictable but non-free branch each iteration.
observable_signals: profiler reports BR_MISP_RETIRED inside the round function
per-block throughput plateaus despite cache hits
likely_causes: rounds expressed as a Python / C++ ``for`` loop
no #pragma unroll on the inner loop
contraindications: do not unroll past the I-cache size on small cores

payload.name: AES Timing Side Channel
payload.symptom_text: Secret-dependent conditional moves leak whether the key bit was 0 or 1 via branch-predictor state.
observable_signals: wall-clock differs measurably with key value
timing-side-channel test in the verifier fails
likely_causes: ternary / if-else on a secret value in the hot path
no branchless lowering of conditional ops
contraindications: branchless rewrites on every comparison can hurt non-secret-dependent paths

payload.name: AES Early-Exit MAC Compare
payload.symptom_text: memcmp-style tag verification stops at the first mismatch, letting an attacker recover the tag one byte at a time.
observable_signals: verifier flags constant-time compare regression
byte-by-byte wall-clock differential on injected mismatches
likely_causes: tag comparison via stock memcmp / standard equality
contraindications: do not roll your own constant-time compare — use the vetted helper

payload.name: CUDA Global-Memory-Bound Kernel
payload.symptom_text: Hot kernel re-reads the same input element from global memory across many threads; bandwidth, not compute, caps performance.
observable_signals: Nsight reports memory throughput saturated and tensor cores idle
tokens/sec scales with bandwidth, not FLOPs
likely_causes: inputs never promoted to shared memory
contraindications: shared memory is scarce; over-tiling reduces occupancy

payload.name: CUDA Low-Occupancy Kernel
payload.symptom_text: Conservative block size leaves the SM underutilised; throughput stays well below theoretical peak.
observable_signals: achieved occupancy < 50% on Nsight
register spill warnings absent (compute under-used)
contraindications: do not hard-code the tuned value across SM counts

payload.name: CUDA Kernel-Launch Overhead
payload.symptom_text: Two kernels chained back-to-back pay double launch cost and round-trip activations through global memory.
observable_signals: wall-clock dominated by launch latency on small batches
activations written then immediately re-read
likely_causes: softmax + matmul left as separate kernels
contraindications: fusion raises register pressure — watch for spill

payload.name: CUDA Load/Compute Serialised
payload.symptom_text: Synchronous global → shared loads stall the warp and leave tensor cores idle between tiles.
observable_signals: Nsight shows compute SOL much lower than memory SOL
no overlap between load and MMA stages on the timeline
likely_causes: blocking ldg / __ldcs in the inner loop
no warp specialization or async pipeline
contraindications: warp specialization requires sm_80+; falls back hurts older devices

payload.name: CUDA Blocking Global Load
payload.symptom_text: Synchronous ldg / __ldcs stall the warp; compute cannot overlap with the next tile's load.
observable_signals: profiler reports memory stalls between MMA stages
contraindications: async copies require careful wait_group fences

payload.name: Scheduler Arrival-Order Bias
payload.symptom_text: Baseline schedules in input order, leaving slack that any priority sort would recover.
observable_signals: makespan exceeds known lower bound on standard instances
utilization on bottleneck machine well below 100%
likely_causes: no sorting / priority pass on the job list
contraindications: static priority rules struggle under high resource contention

payload.name: Scheduler Missing Priority Rule
payload.symptom_text: Ad-hoc dispatch lacks a named priority (SPT / EDD / LPT / slack), so behaviour does not scale with instance size.
observable_signals: performance gap widens with instance size
ablation shows random priority beats the baseline
likely_causes: dispatch loop picks the first ready job verbatim
contraindications: do not freeze the priority weights at the values that tuned one instance

payload.name: Scheduler Greedy Dispatch Bias
payload.symptom_text: Greedy ready-list selection without a named rule loses tail-end optimisations available to Johnson / FFD.
observable_signals: schedule looks good early but late-job tardiness explodes
same instance solved in more dispatch decisions than necessary
likely_causes: dispatch implemented inline without a named-rule abstraction

payload.name: Scheduler Dependency Violations
payload.symptom_text: Schedules emit infeasible orderings because precedence / resource caps are checked post-hoc rather than enforced during dispatch.
observable_signals: verifier reports precedence violation
repair pass needed before every submission
likely_causes: precedence / resource constraints not propagated into the eligibility test
contraindications: do not let repair loops grow without bound — fix the source

payload.fix_summary: Successful runs insert finiteness / range checks at the boundary of their solver so a NaN or invalid output is caught before the evaluator returns 'infeasible'.
preconditions: The candidate artifact compiles and runs without errors on the baseline evaluator.
implementation_steps: - Add a finiteness / range check at the boundary: added boundary / finiteness check on intermediate value.

```python
# Finiteness + range guard at the function boundary.
if not np.all(np.isfinite(out)):
    raise ValueError("non-finite output")
out = np.clip(out, lo, hi)
```
code_targets: The whole editable artifact is the patch site.
expected_verifier_signals: feasibility rate stays high even on novel candidate configurations.
anti_patterns: Do not embed concrete tuning values from another task's baseline archive — see plan §3.5.

payload.fix_summary: A structured optimizer with no wall-clock guard can run past the evaluator timeout and lose the run entirely.
preconditions: The candidate artifact compiles and runs without errors on the baseline evaluator.
implementation_steps: - Gate the search loop on a wall-clock deadline: added wall-clock time budget guard.

```python
import time
DEADLINE = time.time() + WALLCLOCK_BUDGET
while time.time() < DEADLINE:
    ...   # search step
```
code_targets: Locate every `while` / `for` loop that controls the search iteration budget; treat its guard expression as the patch site.
expected_verifier_signals: evaluator returncode stays 0 even when the search explores deep alternatives.
anti_patterns: Do not embed concrete tuning values from another task's baseline archive — see plan §3.5.

payload.fix_summary: Final controller command is not bounded before it reaches the actuator interface.
preconditions: Observable signal: command magnitude tracks raw PID output
Observable signal: actuator saturation flag never set by driver
Symbols present in the editable artifact: `T_cmd`, `desired_pitch`
implementation_steps: - Clamp the controller output before it leaves the function: added output clamp on desired_pitch; added output clamp on T_cmd.

```python
# Clamp the controller output BEFORE the actuator-lag filter.
out = max(min_value, min(max_value, out))
# or numpy:
out = np.clip(out, -bound, bound)
```
code_targets: Search the editable artifact for these identifiers and treat their definitions as the patch site: `T_cmd`, `desired_pitch`.
expected_verifier_signals: feasibility stays valid even on aggressive maneuvers; thrust / torque never exceeds its bound.
anti_patterns: do not implement clamp inside the integrator step — keep it on the output
clamps must be applied *before* the actuator-lag filter, not after, or the command will spike at the next sample
do not implement clamp inside the integrator step — keep it on the output

payload.fix_summary: Synchronous ldg / __ldcs stall the warp; compute cannot overlap with the next tile's load.

payload.fix_summary: Hot kernel re-reads the same input element from global memory across many threads; bandwidth, not compute, caps performance.
preconditions: Observable signal: Nsight reports memory throughput saturated and tensor cores idle

implementation_steps: - Promoted reused inputs into shared-memory tiles before the inner reduction.
_(no patch shape inferred — see Next Experiment)_
code_targets: The whole editable artifact is the patch site.
expected_verifier_signals: tokens-per-sec or GB/s climbs; correctness vs reference kernel stays within tolerance.
anti_patterns: shared memory is scarce; over-tiling reduces occupancy
shared memory is a scarce resource — over-tiling forces low occupancy and may regress.  Tile size must respect ``cudaDeviceGetAttribute(maxSharedMemoryPerBlock)``.
tile boundaries must guard against masked / partial loads or you'll silently read garbage on the edge.
shared memory is scarce; over-tiling reduces occupancy

hard_constraints: Only modify optimization logic inside `scripts/init.py` between `EVOLVE-BLOCK-START` and `EVOLVE-BLOCK-END`.
Keep function signatures unchanged:
load_cases(case_file=None)
simulate(params, case)
baseline_solve(case, max_sim_calls=..., simulate_fn=...)
solve(case, max_sim_calls=..., simulate_fn=...)
Candidate parameters are normalized laser-power control knots and must remain in [0, 1].
Optimize mean thermal-control loss across the real toolpath layers extracted from the upstream repository.
Do not modify `verification/evaluator.py` or files under `references/original/`.
payload.baseline_description: The upstream project `differentiable-simulation-am` studies additive-manufacturing process optimization with differentiable simulation. Its public repository contains a real build geometry (`0.k`), a real toolpath (`toolpath.crs`), and a Ta

hard_constraints: Only modify `scripts/init.py`.
Preserve the public entrypoint, function signatures, and output contract expected by `frontier_eval/evaluator.py`.
Do not modify benchmark assets, documentation, references, verification code, runtime helpers, tests, or `frontier_eval/` metadata.
If the task produces named outputs such as `submission.json`, `results.txt`, `solution.json`, or `prediction.h5ad`, keep the expected filename and schema unchanged.
Prioritize validity and correctness before optimization.
payload.baseline_description: Aerodynamic pressure on a car surface varies with driving conditions. Because sensors are costly and limited, the goal is to place a small number of sensors on the surface that allow accurate reconstruction of the full pressure field.

hard_constraints: Candidate file is `scripts/init.py` and must output `submission.json`.
`submission.json` must be a JSON object with numeric keys:
wing_span_m
wing_area_m2
fuselage_length_m
fuselage_diameter_m
motor_power_kw
battery_mass_kg
cruise_speed_mps
All design values must stay within bounds in `references/mission_config.json`.
Feasibility requires all margins >= 0:

payload.baseline_description: Estimate BER for Hamming(127,120) over AWGN in a rare-event regime.

safety_constraints: cartesian velocity below speed-limit when near human
foot contact force within structural limit

expected_signals: makespan reported as a positive float
score reported as a positive float

payload.name: Unhandled Collision Contact
payload.symptom_text: A robot link makes unintended contact with the environment and the controller keeps driving the same trajectory through the obstacle.
observable_signals: contact force > F_max sustained for multiple control steps
trajectory tracking continues past first contact event
no replan trigger fired after collision detection
likely_causes: no collision response handler subscribed to contact stream
safety margin set below sensor noise floor
contraindications: do not just retry the same trajectory — replan with the updated world model

payload.name: Joint Limit Breach
payload.symptom_text: A joint position or torque crosses the URDF / controller limit because the planner emitted an out-of-range setpoint and the runtime did not clamp it.
observable_signals: joint angle reports > qmax or < qmin for one or more steps
torque command exceeds torque_max at the affected joint
controller error flag raised at the joint level
likely_causes: trajectory generator did not respect URDF joint limits
IK solver returned an out-of-range solution that was sent verbatim
missing post-IK joint-limit clamp in the runtime
contraindications: do not just clamp inside the low-level driver — fix the planner so the limit is respected upstream

payload.name: Sensor Spike or Dropout
payload.symptom_text: A sensor channel returns a value far outside its expected envelope (spike) or no value at all (dropout) and the value flows into the state estimator unfiltered.
observable_signals: sensor reading > 5σ from running mean for a single step
consecutive frames of missing measurements not flagged
state estimate jumps in lock-step with the bad reading
likely_causes: no median / Hampel filter on the sensor pipeline
no dropout detector raising a measurement_valid flag
estimator trusts every sample equally regardless of innovation
contraindications: do not blindly average across an outlier — flag it and let the planner decide

payload.name: Safety Stop Without Recovery Path
payload.symptom_text: Safety controller triggers a halt and the runtime has no defined supervised-resume path; the agent retries the offending command without operator clearance.
observable_signals: safety_stop event followed by repeated identical command retries
no operator-clearance acknowledgement recorded before resumption
post-halt state divergent from pre-halt plan assumptions
likely_causes: missing supervised-resume protocol in the runtime
agent treats safety_stop as a transient error
no state-resync step between halt and resume
contraindications: do not auto-resume after a safety halt without operator acknowledgement

payload.fix_summary: On a collision event, abort the in-flight trajectory, re-snapshot the environment, and replan from the safe-stop pose before resuming.
preconditions: collision detector emits a contact event with location / normal
a planner exists that can re-solve from the safe-stop pose
implementation_steps: - Subscribe to the collision/contact stream and gate command publication on a `collision_clear` flag.
- On contact, push a safe-stop pose and refresh the obstacle map before calling the planner again.

```python
# Pseudo-loop
if collision_event:
    runtime.publish_safe_stop()
    world_model.refresh()
    plan = planner.replan(from_pose=runtime.current_pose())
```
code_targets: collision response handler
replanner entry point
expected_verifier_signals: no second contact event on the same obstacle within the same episode
trajectory completion rate recovers after the replan
anti_patterns: Retrying the failing trajectory without updating the world model.

payload.fix_summary: Apply the URDF joint limit envelope at the planner output, not just at the low-level driver — the planner sees the constraint and uses it to pick feasible IK branches.
preconditions: URDF joint limits are available to the planner
the planner can backtrack when a candidate setpoint is infeasible
implementation_steps: - Inject the joint limit set into the IK solver's constraint list.
- Reject any candidate trajectory whose first violating step falls inside the limit envelope plus a safety margin.
code_targets: IK solver constraint set
trajectory feasibility check
expected_verifier_signals: joint_limit_violation event count drops to zero across the suite
no torque saturation events at the affected joint within the planning horizon
anti_patterns: Clamping silently inside the low-level driver and hiding the bug upstream.

payload.fix_summary: Wrap each noisy sensor channel in a Hampel / rolling-median filter that rejects single-step >5σ outliers, and emit a measurement_valid flag the estimator can downweight.
preconditions: a rolling-window of recent sensor samples is available
the estimator can consume a per-sample validity flag
implementation_steps: - Replace direct sensor reads with a Hampel filter that returns `(value, is_outlier)`.
- Have the estimator multiply the innovation by `0.0 if is_outlier else 1.0`.

```python
v, outlier = hampel_filter(channel, window=21, k=3.0)
if outlier:
    innovation_weight = 0.0
```
code_targets: sensor preprocessing layer
estimator innovation weighting
expected_verifier_signals: state-estimate variance no longer correlates with single-sample sensor spikes
downstream control jitter drops measurably
anti_patterns: Averaging across the outlier window — washes out the spike but also the real signal.

payload.fix_summary: Define an explicit supervised-resume protocol: after a safety stop, the agent must request an operator acknowledgement and re-sync state before the next command is published.
preconditions: an operator-clearance channel exists (UI button / signed token / supervisor service)
the state estimator can re-sync from sensor truth on demand
implementation_steps: - On `safety_stop`, transition the runtime to a `HALTED` state and refuse to publish further commands.
- Require an `operator_clear` event before transitioning to `RESUMING`, then run a state re-sync, then resume.

```python
if event == 'safety_stop':
    runtime.state = 'HALTED'
# blocking — waits for operator
await operator_clear_event()
state.resync()
runtime.state = 'RESUMING'
```
code_targets: runtime state machine
operator-clearance subscriber
expected_verifier_signals: no command publication between safety_stop and operator_clear in the trace
post-resume state error within tolerance of pre-halt plan assumptions
anti_patterns: Treating safety_stop as a transient error and retrying the same command.

payload.fix_summary: Replace open-loop trajectory following with an MPC loop that re-solves the optimisation every dt using the latest measured state, so plan and trajectory never drift far apart.
preconditions: the optimisation problem solves within one control period
a state estimator publishes a fresh measurement every dt
implementation_steps: - Replace the static trajectory follower with an MPC loop bounded by horizon H and step dt.
- At each step: read measured state, re-solve the QP, publish the first command, discard the rest.

```python
for _ in range(steps):
    x = state_estimator.read()
    u = mpc.solve(x, horizon=H, dt=dt)
    runtime.publish(u[0])
```
code_targets: trajectory follower loop
MPC solver wrapper
expected_verifier_signals: position error stops growing monotonically
tracking error stays bounded across the full episode
anti_patterns: Stiffening feed-forward gains without addressing the open-loop drift root cause.

payload.fix_summary: Cap the per-layer KV cache at a sliding window of N tokens and evict the oldest entries on each forward pass; keep an optional global-attention sink for long-context recall.
preconditions: the attention kernel supports a variable-length KV cache
the planner can tolerate a bounded context window
implementation_steps: - Fix `KV_WINDOW = N`.  On each forward, drop entries beyond the window.
- Optionally retain a fixed 'sink' prefix of K tokens that never evicts.

```python
if kv.size > KV_WINDOW:
    kv = kv[-KV_WINDOW:]
# optional sink: kv = torch.cat([sink, kv[-(KV_WINDOW - SINK_LEN):]], dim=0)
```
code_targets: KV-cache accumulator
attention forward pass
expected_verifier_signals: GPU memory rises sub-linearly with sequence length
throughput stays stable past the previous OOM threshold
anti_patterns: Cutting batch size to mask the leak — only delays the OOM by one batch.

payload.fix_summary: Apply per-step gradient clipping (`clip_grad_norm_`) at a tuned threshold before the optimiser step, plus an automatic learning-rate halving when the clipped norm fires for K consecutive steps.
preconditions: the optimiser exposes a step hook callable between backward and step
training step is the bottleneck (so the clip cost is amortised)
implementation_steps: - After `loss.backward()`, call `torch.nn.utils.clip_grad_norm_(model.parameters(), max_norm=GRAD_CLIP).`
- Track how often the clip fires; if it fires for K steps in a row, halve `lr`.

```python
loss.backward()
norm = torch.nn.utils.clip_grad_norm_(model.parameters(), max_norm=GRAD_CLIP)
if norm > GRAD_CLIP:
    consecutive_clips += 1
    if consecutive_clips >= K:
        for g in optim.param_groups: g['lr'] *= 0.5
else:
    consecutive_clips = 0
optim.step()
```
code_targets: training step
lr scheduler hook
expected_verifier_signals: grad-norm distribution capped at GRAD_CLIP
no NaN in loss across a fixed-length training run
anti_patterns: Catching NaN after the fact and zeroing it out — the optimiser moment buffers are already corrupted.
